main.name: Ayyah Saleh
main.occupation: CIPP-US | J.D. Candidate | Data Privacy, IP & Emerging Tech Law
main.bio: Currently pursuing a Juris Doctor at The George Washington University Law School, with a CIPP/US certification and coursework in AI safety, ethics, and society. Extensive experience includes legal internships at Dow Jones and FlexAI, contributing to privacy compliance and regulatory research. Previous roles as Law and Billing Clerk at Crimmigration Inc and Grant Manager at a National Civil Rights Nonprofit honed skills in legal documentation, client relations, and grant management. Proficient in LexisNexis, CCPA, and HIPAA, with fluency in Arabic and Spanish.

education.school: The George Washington University Law School
education.description: Data Privacy, IP, and Emerging Tech Law focus
Coursework in AI safety, ethics, and society

work.title: Summer Legal Intern
work.description: Legal internship at Dow Jones in the New York City Metropolitan Area.

work.title: Legal Intern
work.description: Contributing to privacy compliance and regulatory research over an 8-month internship.

work.title: Summer Law Clerk
work.description: Performing document review for litigation defense matters. Analyzing and researching issues relating to tort defense and bodily injury. Examining case strategy to determine best client outcomes.

work.title: Paralegal and Billing Clerk
work.description: Managed account and billing services for a law firm with over 600 clients, including monthly client invoicing and IOLTA billing. Provided professional translation services for clients (Arabic, Spanish, Dari, and Farsi). Prepared multiple adjustments of status applications. Wrote Multiple Freedom of Information Act Requests for clients in immigration detention.

work.title: Grant Manager
work.description: Drafted and submitted federal and state grant applications. Project Management for immigrant advocacy networks. Founded Interstate Immigrant Outreach Initiatives. Sacramento County, California.

projects.title: Data Privacy & AI Governance
projects.description: Research on the intersection of AI regulation, data privacy law (CCPA/HIPAA), and emerging tech compliance.
projects.category: Research on AI regulation and data privacy law

projects.title: Cryptocurrency Regulation

projects.title: IP & Investment Policy
